metadata.name: Learn > Installation screen
metadata.description: Verifies the one-line-bootstrap installation page: hero + CTA CodeBlock, 3 install-target cards, 5 prereq rows (required/optional tiers), three static install steps with CodeBlocks, four expandable detail sections (partial update / custom path / individual install / uninstall), six individually-expandable troubleshoot rows, five related-link rows, the 'next: Hello World' CTA, and the header language toggle. Every assertion traces back to a section of learn/installation.spec.json.

cases.name: renders_hero
cases.description: On mount the hero headline (learn_installation_title bound to 'headline' key -> 'Install JsonUI in one line') is visible, and the hero CTA CodeBlock renders with the bootstrap curl one-liner. Traces to the layout's header View + hero CTA CodeBlock (literal code per spec notes). Text narrowing is used for the code body because CodeBlock elements have no stable per-instance DOM id.

cases.name: renders_three_install_target_cards
cases.description: The 'what gets installed' Collection renders exactly 3 InstallTargetCard cells seeded by onAppear: jsonui-cli / jsonui-mcp-server / Agents + Skills + Rules. Each card's title is resolved via StringManager (card_cli_title / card_mcp_title / card_agents_title). Traces to stateManagement.uiVariables.installTargets and the spec notes 'installTargets count = 3'. Per-cell ids are not guaranteed stable, so we assert by the titles rendered inside the parent Collection.

cases.name: renders_five_prereq_rows
cases.description: The prerequisites Collection renders 5 PrereqRow cells (git+curl, Node.js >= 20, npm, Ruby >= 2.7, Python 3 >= 3.10). The cell layout shows a tier badge (REQUIRED / OPTIONAL) resolved from prereq_required_badge / prereq_optional_badge; at least one of each tier is present in the v1 catalog (git/node/npm required; ruby/python optional). Traces to stateManagement.uiVariables.prerequisites and the spec's PrereqRow custom type (requiredTier). Text narrowing via the parent Collection id because cells have no guaranteed DOM ids.

cases.name: renders_three_steps_with_code
cases.description: The three static inline step Views (step_1 / step_2 / step_3) each render their number badge + title + body + a CodeBlock. Step 2's CodeBlock carries the zshrc PATH snippet containing '~/.jsonui-cli/sjui_tools/bin'; step 3's CodeBlock carries the literal 'Read CLAUDE.md' prompt. Traces to the spec notes (steps are static inline Views in the layout, not a Collection) and to the exact code literals the spec declares.

cases.name: partial_update_section_expands_on_tap
cases.description: On mount partialUpdateBodyVisibility defaults to 'gone' (expandedIds is empty), so installation_partial_update_body is absent from the DOM. Tapping the Partial update header (by its spec-resolved title 'Partial update (advanced)') invokes onTogglePartialUpdate -> onToggleExpand('partial_update'), which adds the id to expandedIds; the displayLogic entry flips partialUpdateBodyVisibility='visible' and the body mounts with its 'partial_update_body' copy and the partial-update CodeBlock. Traces to stateManagement.displayLogic[partial_update] and stateManagement.uiVariables.expandedIds.

cases.name: custom_path_section_expands_on_tap
cases.description: Mirror of the partial-update case for the Custom install paths section. Default customPathBodyVisibility='gone' so installation_custom_path_body is not in the DOM; tapping the header invokes onToggleCustomPath -> onToggleExpand('custom_path'), flipping customPathBodyVisibility='visible'. The body's 'custom_path_body' copy and the JSONUI_CLI_DIR/JSONUI_MCP_DIR CodeBlock render. Traces to stateManagement.displayLogic[custom_path].

cases.name: individual_install_section_expands_on_tap
cases.description: Mirror of the partial-update case for the Install components individually section. Default individualInstallBodyVisibility='gone'; tapping the header invokes onToggleIndividualInstall -> onToggleExpand('individual_install'), which flips individualInstallBodyVisibility='visible' and mounts the body with its per-component curl snippets. Traces to stateManagement.displayLogic[individual_install].

cases.name: uninstall_section_expands_on_tap
cases.description: Mirror of the partial-update case for the Uninstall section. Default uninstallBodyVisibility='gone'; tapping the header invokes onToggleUninstall -> onToggleExpand('uninstall'), which flips uninstallBodyVisibility='visible' and mounts the body with its removal commands (jsonui-mcp-server uninstall.sh / rm -rf ~/.jsonui-cli / rm -rf .claude CLAUDE.md). Traces to stateManagement.displayLogic[uninstall].

cases.name: troubleshoot_rows_expand_individually
cases.description: Each TroubleshootRow cell uses onClick -> onToggleExpand(row.id) and binds its cause+fix body's visibility via its own rowVisibility prop (computed by the VM from expandedIds). Tapping one row's symptom expands only that row. Tapping a second row expands it too (both are open simultaneously because expandedIds is a Set). Tapping the first row's symptom again removes it from expandedIds, collapsing only that row while the second stays open. This exercises the Set semantics of expandedIds and the self-contained cell-level toggle pattern described in the spec notes. Text narrowing is used throughout because troubleshoot cells share the same cells/troubleshoot_row layout and have no stable per-instance DOM id.

cases.name: related_links_render_five_rows
cases.description: The related-links Collection renders exactly 5 RelatedLink cells seeded by onAppear. Each cell includes a Label bound to the cell's url field, so the five spec-declared URLs (/tools/cli, /tools/mcp, /tools/agents, /learn/hello-world, /tools/mcp/tools/get-data-source) all appear inside the Collection's rendered subtree. Traces to stateManagement.uiVariables.relatedLinks and the spec's transitions ('v1 destinations from this page').

cases.name: related_link_tap_navigates
cases.description: Tapping the /tools/cli related-link row invokes the cell's onClick -> onNavigate('/tools/cli') -> router.push('/tools/cli'). The Installation tree unmounts (the /tools/cli route currently returns Next.js's 404 page, so the root is replaced rather than augmented). Asserting that learn_installation_root is no longer visible captures the navigation intent independent of whether the destination page exists yet. Traces to stateManagement.eventHandlers.onNavigate and transitions.trigger='onNavigate(url)'. Text narrowing is used because related-link cells share the same cell layout and have no stable per-instance DOM id.

cases.name: next_cta_navigates_to_hello_world
cases.description: Tapping learn_installation_next_cta invokes onNavigateHelloWorld, wired by initializeEventHandlers to () => this.onNavigate('/learn/hello-world') -> router.push('/learn/hello-world'). The Installation tree unmounts in favour of the Hello World page, so learn_installation_root is no longer visible. Traces to stateManagement.eventHandlers.onNavigate and userActions 'Tap the next: Hello World CTA'.

cases.name: toc_renders_and_localizes
cases.description: The TableOfContents inside learn_installation_toc_wrap renders its title ('On this page') and at least two canonical section labels (Prerequisites / Troubleshooting) seeded by the VM from TOC_ROW_DEFS. Toggling language flips the title to 'このページ' and 'Prerequisites' to '前提条件' — proving that tocTitle and tocItems[].label flow through StringManager on the language change. Traces to stateManagement.uiVariables.tocTitle + tocItems and to the spec's on-page TableOfContents contract.

cases.name: language_toggle_re_seeds
cases.description: StringManager.language defaults to 'en' on first render, so learn_installation_title resolves to the English headline 'Install JsonUI in one line'. Tapping learn_installation_lang_toggle invokes onToggleLanguage, which flips StringManager to 'ja' and calls onAppear() again; every @string-bound Label re-resolves in Japanese. The title flips to its ja copy ('JsonUI をワンライナーで導入'), which contains the distinctive 'ワンライナー' fragment. This implicitly verifies that onToggleLanguage -> StringManager.setLanguage('ja') -> onAppear() re-seeds every Collection in the new language. Traces to stateManagement.eventHandlers.onToggleLanguage and stateManagement.uiVariables.currentLanguage plus userActions 'Toggle language'.
